subject: Re: A Memorial Day Tribute to Guess Who????
body:
I'm laughing because in Lafayette HS, there was a guy who was skinny that
reminded us of Colonel Bleep, but for some reason the name got perverted
and we called him Gleep.
I used to see him around SOHO (he bacame an attorney and had an office
here)  I would be walking down the street and spot him across the street
and of course yell out HEY GLEEP!
Groovy knows him too.

I had forgotten about Dr. Destructo.   Yeah

EarthLink Revolves Around You.

[Original Message]
From: STEVEN KIRSON skir3@msn.com
To: izmo@mindspring.com
Date: 5/21/2008 2:26:12 PM
Subject: A Memorial Day Tribute to Guess Who????

Mark:

I hope everything is going well.

Another national holiday looms days away and a tribute to one of the
great
heroes of WWll has been created by Larry Kirson.  I'll give you two
guesses
to whom this tribute is made to and if it take more then one guess I will
be
surprised.

See attachment above.

Our town is sponsoring  a "Porch Tour" of the Victorian homes in the
neighborhood on Memorial Day.  There will be horse drawn carriages and
barber shop quartets (Frankie the barber is the baritone and he is
bringing
his special magazines from the back of his shop).  If you have the kids
that
day they might (or might not) enjoy it.  You are welcome to come if you
do
not have your kids.  This event is from 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM.  We will
have a
barbecue after.

Steve

From: Larry Kirson glalarry@optonline.net
To: Steve Kirson skir3@msn.com
Subject: Why we celebrate Memorial Day
Date: Wed, 21 May 2008 12:35:14 -0400
